headline: Apple stock target raised as Citi sees continued market share gains
summary: Investing.com -- Citi has increased its price target on Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) to $365 and maintained its Buy rating on the stock in a note on Monday, citing the company's ability to gain market share in a challenging devices environment and its capacity to implement selective price increases to protect margins.

headline: Wall Street Mounts Pushback on Trillion-Dollar Stablecoin Boom
summary: (Bloomberg) -- For years, banks largely watched stablecoins evolve from a niche cryptocurrency product into a payments network moving tens of trillions of dollars annually. headline: Bank Earnings Should Wow Wall Street. What's Driving Those Bottom Lines.
summary: The coming batch of report cards from America’s largest banks is set to reflect that Wall Street’s core businesses are doing exceptionally well, driven by a Main Street that’s doing well enough.  Strong equities-trading and investment-banking activity this spring should propel earnings at Wells Fargo, Citigroup, JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, and Goldman Sachs, which each report within hours of each other on Tuesday.  Morgan Stanley, U.S. Bancorp, and other lenders are scheduled to report in the days after.

content.title: Bank Earnings Should Wow Wall Street. What's Driving Those Bottom Lines.

Morgan Stanley, U.S. Bancorp, and other lenders are scheduled to report in the days after.

headline: Does Citi’s Tokenized USD Clearing Breakthrough Deepen the Digital Transformation Narrative for Citigroup (C)?
summary: Earlier this month, The Siam Commercial Bank became the first client to go live with Citigroup Inc.’s integrated 24/7 USD Clearing and Citi Token Services, using a private blockchain and tokenized deposits to enable always-on cross-border payments across more than 50 markets. This early adoption not only accelerates Thailand’s digital financial infrastructure but also highlights how Citi’s tokenized deposit network could reshape institutional payment operations across banks and...

headline: Big Banks Poised for Higher Profits as Earnings Season Kicks Off
summary: The four largest U.S. banks and Goldman Sachs are poised to report higher profits from a year ago thanks to buoyant equity markets, a solid economy, and merger and IPO activity. JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citigroup, Wells Fargo, and Goldman are due to release their second-quarter earnings results on Tuesday in an unusually packed morning for bank investors.

JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citigroup, Wells Fargo, and Goldman are due to release their second-quarter earnings results on Tuesday in an unusually packed morning for bank investors.
